target: PRESS-TUNE: multi-stage progressive FT with stage-specific optimizer/data/temperature for capability fractionation

results.title: First Cold Pressed Olive Oil
results.notes: Source-domain olive press article; no LLM training relevance.

results.title: Fine-Tuning LLMs with Sequential Instructions (NAACL 2025)
results.notes: Sequential instruction-tuning across stages; matches progressive multi-stage FT idea though without explicit stage-specific optimizer/temperature framing.

results.title: Improved SFT to Mitigate Catastrophic Forgetting (2025/6)
results.notes: Multi-stage SFT framing for forgetting mitigation; partial overlap but not capability fractionation.

results.title: GLPFT: Global-to-Local Progressive Fine-Tuning (2025/2)
results.notes: 2-stage progressive FT with sub-task partitioning; directly anticipated as prior art in candidate's novelty_claim.

results.title: Multi-Stage Capability Fractionation via Stage-Specific FT (2026/3)
results.notes: Direct match: stage-specific data and optimizer extracting capability fractions; near-identical framing to PRESS-TUNE.

results.title: Domain Adaptation FT: scaling + merging (npj 2025)
results.notes: Synergistic capabilities pipeline with scaling/merging; functional overlap on multi-stage capability composition.

results.title: Sequential Multi-Stage FT for Capability Decomposition (2025/9)
results.notes: Capability decomposition across sequential FT stages; strongly matches fractionation framing.

results.title: CPT-SFT-DPO Capability-Specific Fractionation Pipeline (2026/4)
results.notes: Explicit CPT->SFT->DPO pipeline with stage-specific capability extraction; matches PRESS-TUNE's progressive SFT-to-DPO-to-RLHF chain.

rationale: Multiple direct matches (ranks 5, 7, 8) explicitly describe multi-stage progressive FT with stage-specific data/optimizer for capability fractionation. GLPFT (rank 4) and sequential instruction FT (rank 2) provide additional prior art. The candidate itself acknowledges in novelty_claim that the underlying mechanism is mainstream. Stage-specific temperature is the only minor differentiator but is not load-bearing.
